title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Harriet Lymon informs Mrs. Oliver that she and a man named James Jenkins witnessed Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith sign a codicil to her will, confirming the signature's authenticity. Lymon notes that Jenkins moved to Australia eighteen months ago and is no longer local. Expressing guilt for her previous silence, Lymon claims a child named Joey witnessed Miss Olga killing Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith. She observes that Miss Olga appeared smug regarding the potential inheritance but fled suddenly when the police arrived. Mrs. Oliver advises Lymon to report this information to Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith's lawyer and promises to speak on her behalf.

Hercule Poirot later joins Mrs. Oliver to discuss the case. Poirot emphasizes the importance of "dates," noting that the current murder is rooted in a past event. He explains that a murder occurring years ago, witnessed by a girl, led to that girl being killed four days ago. Poirot states that his investigation has yielded facts arranged by date, though he notes that no one believes the testimony of Joyce Reynolds. Regarding the disputed will, Poirot suggests that while people suspect the foreign exchange student, a different forger existed in the village. This unknown forger died approximately two years ago after being killed by a knife following a dispute involving jealousy and a girl. Poirot suggests these seemingly independent incidents may be interconnected.
